subject: Social and Political Life - II
chapter_name: Growing Up as Boys and Girls
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze gender as a socio-cultural construct through comparative ethnographic studies (Samoa and Madhya Pradesh)
Evaluate the structural devaluation of 'reproductive labor' within the patriarchal economic framework
Critically examine the 'Double Burden' phenomenon and its impact on female workforce participation
Investigate the intersectionality of class and gender in the lives of domestic workers
Deconstruct the 'Hidden Curriculum' of gender socialization in childhood play and spatial mobility
Synthesize the relationship between Constitutional mandates of equality and State-led institutional interventions
Formulate arguments on the necessity of 'Care-giving' as a recognized economic activity

lesson_plan.title: Sociological Analysis: Samoa vs. Madhya Pradesh
topics_covered: Ethnographic study of Samoan Islands (1920s): Freedom from formal gendered childhood
Spatial Politics in 1960s Madhya Pradesh: The street as a 'gendered' space
The shift from 'care-receiver' to 'care-giver' in indigenous societies
teaching_strategy: Comparative analysis of 'Socialization' models (Formal vs. Informal)
Discussion on 'Spatial Agency': How girls and boys perceive public vs. private domains
Anthropological inquiry: Why did Samoan children assume responsibility earlier?
student_activities: Draft a comparative report on 'Spatial Mobility' of girls in 1960s MP vs. modern urban India
Analyze the 'Group Movement' of girls as a defense mechanism against social gaze
assessment: HOTS: 'Is the street a neutral space?'
Critical analysis of the transition points in Samoan childhood

lesson_plan.title: Deconstructing Socialization: Toys and Agency
topics_covered: Toys as ideological tools: Creating 'Different Futures'
The psychological impact of gender-coded communication
Subtle conditioning: Soft-spokenness vs. Assertiveness
teaching_strategy: Semiotic analysis of toy packaging and marketing
Discussion on how socialization limits vocational aspirations
Inquiry into 'Gender Roles' as a mechanism for social order
student_activities: Analyze three advertisements for children's products and identify the 'gendered script' being used
Debate: 'Can gender-neutral parenting exist in a gendered society?'
assessment: Reflection paper: 'Toys as a prelude to professional segregation'

lesson_plan.title: The Economics of Housework: Invisible & Unpaid
topics_covered: The 'Invisible' nature of domestic labor
Structural Devaluation: Why 'Home-making' isn't counted in GDP
Case Study: The 'One-Day Strike' – disrupting the household equilibrium
teaching_strategy: Economic analysis: Calculating the 'Shadow Price' of daily domestic chores
Critiquing the 'Naturalness' of care-giving being a female-only domain
Analyzing the physical and mental toll of 'invisible' tasks
student_activities: Create a 'Labor Audit' of a household for one week, assigning hypothetical wages to each task
Analyze the comic strip: How does the father's perception of 'work' exclude domesticity?
assessment: Analytical response: 'Explain the systemic reasons for the devaluation of housework'
Definition and significance of 'Care-giving' as labor

lesson_plan.title: The Informal Sector: Domestic Workers
topics_covered: The life of Melani: A study in marginalization and class-gender intersection
The 'Dignity of Labor' deficit in domestic help
Lack of legal safeguards and the 'Employer-Employee' power dynamic
teaching_strategy: Sociological inquiry: Why is domestic work primarily done by women and the marginalized?
Discussion on 'Living Standards' vs. 'Service Provided' in the story of Melani
Exploring the 'Master-Servant' narrative vs. professional labor rights
student_activities: Conduct a mock interview with an imaginary domestic worker to identify 'Rights Violations'
Write a policy proposal for the 'Social Security of Domestic Workers'
assessment: Short essay: 'The intersection of poverty and gender in domestic labor'
Explaining why housework is 'physically demanding' using specific examples

lesson_plan.title: The Double Burden and the State's Role
topics_covered: The 'Double Burden': Theoretical framework and real-world data
Article 15: Constitutional mandate vs. Social reality
Inequality as a 'Systemic Issue' rather than an 'Individual Choice'
teaching_strategy: Analyzing 'Time-Use Survey' data to visualize the Double Burden
Discussion on why the State must intervene in the 'Private' sphere of the home
Mapping the transition from 'Constitutional Rights' to 'Legislative Action'
student_activities: Create an infographic explaining the 'Double Burden' and its impact on women's health
Research: Does the Constitution permit 'special provisions' for women? Identify the clause.
assessment: Defining 'Double Burden' in a sociological context
Logic-based quiz on the role of the State in ensuring equality

lesson_plan.title: Institutional Interventions: Anganwadis and Crèches
topics_covered: The Anganwadi system: Decentralized childcare and nutrition
The Maternity Benefit Act (Crèche provision): Economic and social impact
Enabling Girls' Education through sibling-care alternatives
teaching_strategy: Evaluating the 'Chain Reaction': Crèche $ightarrow$ Mother's Employment $ightarrow$ Daughter's Schooling
Critique of current childcare infrastructure in India
Discussion on 'Institutionalizing Care' to liberate female labor
student_activities: Analyze a case study: How an Anganwadi improved the female literacy rate in a specific village
Draft a letter to the Labor Ministry advocating for stricter Crèche law enforcement
assessment: Explaining the strategic importance of Crèches for girls' education

lesson_plan.title: Synthesis: Towards Gender Equity
topics_covered: Synthesizing Samoa, MP, and contemporary legal frameworks
The concept of 'Equity' vs. 'Equality' in gender
Final mastery of NCERT HOTS questions
teaching_strategy: Advanced Concept Mapping: The cycle of Socialization and Devaluation
Seminar: 'The Future of Care-giving in a Digital Economy'
Review of Assertion-Reasoning patterns
student_activities: Construct an integrated mind map of the entire chapter
assessment: Evaluation of the integrated mind map for conceptual depth

teaching_aids: Social and Political Life - II Textbook
Documentary snippets on Domestic Workers' movements
Global comparison data on 'The Unpaid Work Gap'
teacher_tip: At the hard level, avoid treating this as a 'women's chapter'. Treat it as a 'structure of society' chapter. Focus on how the devaluation of domestic work isn't just a social habit, but an economic loophole that democracy must close.
